sections.a_eyebrow: Type as Hero · Minimal · Professional · Whitespace · Elegant
sections.a_description: The distinctive visual language of Type-Only (No Images) — combining type as hero, minimal, professional elements to create interfaces that are immediately recognizable and purposefully designed. Every color, font choice, and spacing decision works together to evoke the specific mood and cultural context that defines this aesthetic, from the primary accent color to the carefully chosen typography.

This style finds its natural home across websites and applications that value type as hero and minimal design principles. You'll see it on creative portfolios, brand microsites, specialized platforms, and digital experiences where the visual language itself communicates as powerfully as the content — proving that the right aesthetic choice can transform a standard layout into a memorable experience.

a_image_cover.alt: Type-Only (No Images) style use case illustration
Type-Only (No Images) style use case illustration
Type-Only (No Images) style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The distinctive visual aesthetic of Type-Only (No Images) — a design approach that creates unique, memorable interfaces. Background: light (#ffffff). The .inner panel has a clean background with subtle border treatment and appropriate border-radius. Colors: #333333 as the primary accent for interactive elements and highlights. #111111 for headings, #999999 for body text. The aesthetic captures the essence of type-only (no images) through careful color choices, spacing, and visual weight. Set in Spectral — chosen for its visual character that complements this design style. Heading at weight 600-700, 24-28px, line-height 1.2-1.3. Eyebrow at weight 500, 11px, uppercase, 2-3px letter-spacing, color #333333. Body at weight 400, 14-15px, line-height 1.6-1.7, color #999999. Buttons: primary — #333333 background with contrasting text. Secondary: transparent with #333333 border. Alternatives: system-ui (universal fallback), serif stack.
